opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I accidentally ate something with peanuts in it and now my throat feels tight and I'm covered in hives, my EpiPen didn't seem to work properly.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Suspected severe allergic reaction after accidental peanut ingestion, with possible autoinjector failure.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 15 minutes ago, shortly after eating a baked good
symptom_narrative.character: throat tightness, difficulty breathing, whole-body itching and hives
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: lightheadedness, noisy breathing, swelling of the lips
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: known severe peanut allergy since childhood, has had anaphylaxis once before requiring hospital treatment
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: roommate says her voice sounds different and she is struggling to speak in full sentences

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: used one just now but it felt like it may have expired or not worked properly, has a second one available

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: roommate present and assisting, has a second adrenaline autoinjector in the house

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: very frightened, wants reassurance on whether to use the second EpiPen

access_barriers.transport: roommate present but patient may not be safe to transport, breathing difficulty worsening
